note: All templates and customisations as they were BEFORE the 12 June 2026 CAB converged-text update. Frozen copy; mirror of the repo folder “Templates before 12 June/”.

templates.name: Citizens Advice (CAB)
templates.description: For Citizens Advice offices and other advice agencies to hand to clients. Adds Pupil Premium and Child Benefit messaging to the NAWRA structure.

body.hero_subtitle: Important news for parents and carers
body.intro_html: Every child whose family receives Universal Credit can get free school meals.
body.cta_primary_body_html: Sign up before the Autumn term starts so your child gets every free meal they're entitled to.
body.box1_title: If your child currently gets free school meals
body.box1_body_html: From the 2026/27 school year, eligibility will be checked every year. In some areas your local authority will auto-enrol your child — otherwise make sure you claim again so your child keeps their free meals and your school keeps its Pupil Premium funding.
body.box2_title: If you receive Universal Credit but haven't claimed before
body.box2_body_html: Your child is now eligible. Claim from 1 June for the start of September.
body.how_to_intro: About an hour of your time — and two things worth doing:
body.how_to_steps_html:
• Apply for free school meals through your school office, your school's website, or your local council. You'll need: your National Insurance number, your address and postcode, your child's full name and date of birth, and the name of the benefit you receive (Universal Credit or another qualifying benefit).
  • Check you're claiming Child Benefit. Even if one parent earns over £80,000 and the payment is fully clawed back by the High Income Child Benefit Charge, register anyway and tick the box to opt out of payment — it protects the at-home parent's National Insurance record.
body.cta_secondary_body_html: You may still be entitled. A free benefit calculator will tell you if you could qualify for Universal Credit and free school meals.
body.attribution_html: Free to copy, adapt and share · CC BY 4.0
body.box3_title: If your child is in reception, Year 1 or Year 2
body.box3_body_html: All children in reception, Year 1 and Year 2 get free school meals automatically, whatever the family income. If you receive Universal Credit, still tell the school office and register — it's what brings the school its Pupil Premium funding.

facts.eligibility_basis: Receipt of Universal Credit (England, from September 2026)
facts.auto_enrolment_status: Some LAs auto-enrol; others require parents to claim each year.
facts.claim_window_purpose: To be ready for the start of September.
facts.application_route: School- or LA-distributed form; CAB advisers may help families complete.

templates.name: entitledto — LA bespoke
templates.description: LA-customisable version with auto-enrolment messaging. Calculator URL points to the LA-specific entitledto subdomain.

body.hero_subtitle: Important news for parents and carers
body.intro_html: From September 2026, every child whose family receives Universal Credit can get free school meals.
body.cta_primary_title: The government are expanding free school meals and getting everyone to register annually.
body.cta_primary_body_html: Apply before the Autumn term starts so your child doesn't miss out.
body.box1_title: If your child currently gets free school meals
body.box1_body_html: From the 2026/27 school year, eligibility will be checked every year. In some areas your local authority will auto-enrol your child — otherwise make sure you claim again so your child keeps their free meals.
body.box2_title: If you receive Universal Credit but haven't claimed before
body.box2_body_html: Your child is now eligible. Claim from 1 June for the start of September.
body.how_to_steps_html:
• Download the form from your school's website
  • Or pick up a paper copy from the school office
body.cta_secondary_body_html: You may still be entitled. Use the free, independent entitledto calculator to check Universal Credit, free school meals, and 30+ other benefits in under 10 minutes.
body.contact_website: Used by Local Authorities, Housing Associations and Citizens Advice
body.attribution_html: Published by entitledto.co.uk · Free to copy, adapt and share · CC BY 4.0

overrides.hero_title: Free school meals: Lambeths expanded offer
Free school meals: Lambeth's expanded offer
overrides.hero_subtitle: Important news for parents and carers
overrides.intro_html: From September 2026, every Lambeth child whose family receives Universal Credit can get free school meals.
overrides.cta_primary_body_html: Lambeth has been auto-enrolling children for years. Most eligible children will be signed up automatically — but contact your school if you don't hear from us by September.
overrides.box1_title: If your child currently gets free school meals
overrides.box1_body_html: Lambeth checks eligibility every year and auto-enrols most eligible children. If your circumstances have changed, contact your school or the Lambeth Welfare team to make sure your child stays signed up.
overrides.box2_title: If you receive Universal Credit but haven't claimed before
overrides.box2_body_html: Your child is now eligible. Lambeth may auto-enrol you from 1 June — or apply directly through your school for the start of September. Full details: lambeth.gov.uk/freeschoolmeals.
overrides.how_to_steps_html:
• Apply at lambeth.gov.uk/freeschoolmeals
  • Or via your school's website
  • Or pick up a paper copy from the school office
overrides.cta_secondary_body_html: You may still be entitled. Use Lambeth's free benefit calculator to check if you could qualify for Universal Credit and free school meals.

overrides.hero_title: Free school meals: what's changing in Leeds
overrides.hero_subtitle: Important news for parents and carers
overrides.intro_html: From September 2026, every Leeds child whose family receives Universal Credit can get free school meals.
overrides.cta_primary_body_html: Leeds processes applications over the summer. Apply online or through your school so your child is signed up before the Autumn term starts.
overrides.box1_title: If your child currently gets free school meals
overrides.box1_body_html: From 7 September 2026, the current rules and £7,400 earnings limit end. Eligibility will be checked every year — if your situation has changed, contact Welfare and Benefits to make sure your child keeps their free meals.
overrides.box2_title: If you receive Universal Credit but haven't claimed before
overrides.box2_body_html: Your child is now eligible. Apply online from 1 June or download the form — Leeds will process applications over the summer.
overrides.cta_secondary_body_html: You may still be entitled. Use Leeds' free benefit calculator to check if you could qualify for Universal Credit and free school meals.

overrides.hero_title: Free school meals: what's changing in Oxfordshire
overrides.hero_subtitle: Important news for parents and carers
overrides.intro_html: From September 2026, every child whose family receives Universal Credit can get free school meals.
overrides.cta_primary_title: Apply by end of summer term
overrides.cta_primary_body_html: Oxfordshire processes applications over the summer so your child is ready for free meals on the first day of the Autumn term.
overrides.box1_title: If your child currently gets free school meals
overrides.box1_body_html: From the 2026/27 school year, eligibility will be checked every year. Oxfordshire is exploring auto-enrolment — look out for a letter from us, and contact your school if you don't hear by the start of term.
overrides.box2_title: If you receive Universal Credit but haven't claimed before
overrides.box2_body_html: Your child is now eligible. Apply through your school by the end of the summer term. Full details: schools.oxfordshire.gov.uk.
overrides.how_to_steps_html:
• Apply at schools.oxfordshire.gov.uk
  • Or download the form from your school's website
  • Or pick up a paper copy from the school office
overrides.cta_secondary_body_html: You may still be entitled. Use Oxfordshire's free benefit calculator to check if you could qualify for Universal Credit and free school meals.
